title: impatiens capensis f. peasei
intro: Impatiens capensis f. peasei, also known as orange jewelweed, is a unique variety of the jewelweed plant (Impatiens capensis) characterized by its bright orange flowers. This plant is used in herbal medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe skin irritations, such as poison ivy rashes, and reduce the risk of infection. The medicinal properties of Impatiens capensis f. peasei also provide health benefits, including reducing pain, inflammation, and itching, as well as promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and salves, are often used topically to treat skin conditions and internally to support overall health and well-being.
